title: Edit File
description: Create or modify a text file inside the active workspace directory. The primary contract is version-guarded range edits over exact file text, with convenience line operations for common cases. Every real edit writes a checkpoint before changing the file.

operation.description: edit applies transactional range edits. write replaces the entire file, append adds a line-aware block at EOF, replace swaps literal text, set_end_of_line converts document line endings, set_line/replace_line changes one line body while preserving its line ending, replace_lines swaps an inclusive line range, insert_before/after insert whole line blocks around an anchor line, and delete_line/delete_lines remove line ranges.

expected_version.description: Required for every edit. Use the version returned by read_file. Use the literal string "missing" only when intentionally creating a file that does not yet exist.

content.description: Required for write, append, set_line/replace_line, replace_lines, insert_before/insert_after, and their *_line aliases. For append, content is added as a line block at EOF: line endings are normalized to the document EOL, a missing boundary before the appended block is added, and a missing trailing line ending is added. For set_line this is the new line body without a newline. For replace_lines and range edit text, line endings are normalized to the document EOL. For inserts this is a line block; a missing trailing line ending is added automatically, and an empty string inserts a blank line.

edits.description: Required for operation=edit. Applies all edits transactionally against the original document. Positions use 1-based line and 0-based column offsets; ranges are end-exclusive. To include a line break, end the range at column 0 of the next line.

old_text.description: Required for replace operations. The edit is rejected if this text is missing or ambiguous unless replace_all is true.

line.description: 1-based line number for set_line/replace_line, delete_line, insert_before/insert_after, and aliases. insert_before with line 1 may create content in an empty existing file.

start_line.description: 1-based inclusive start line for replace_lines and delete_lines.

end_line.description: 1-based inclusive end line for replace_lines and delete_lines. Defaults to start_line when omitted.

expected_old_text.description: Optional safety check. For set_line and insert anchors, compare with the current line body without the line ending. For replace_lines and delete_lines, compare with the exact targeted range including line endings.

replace_all.description: Only used by replace. When false, ambiguous old_text matches are rejected.

create_if_missing.description: Defaults to true for write and append, false for targeted edits.

selection.summary: Apply version-guarded range, line, or whole-file edits in a workspace-contained file with an automatic checkpoint.
selection.whenToUse: Use when the user asks the AI to modify project files. Read the file first, pass expected_version, and prefer operation=edit for precise range edits. Use line helpers for simple line operations and set_end_of_line only when explicitly changing document EOL. Change the working directory first to set the workspace root, including a mounted or remote project path.
selection.whenNotToUse: Do not use for reading files, editing paths outside the active workspace, binary files, or destructive directory operations.
